newData.title: Chrono-Acoustic Posture Encoding Seat
newData.description: Detecting user posture without physical sensors or cameras. Unlike conventional methods that rely on pressure, strain, or optical sensing, this embodiment encodes posture information by modulating the acoustic resonance frequencies of a multi-chambered seat structure based on internal air displacement patterns caused by the user's weight distribution.

newData.title: Thermo-Gradient Ergonomic Feedback System
newData.description: Leveraging the subtle thermoregulatory responses of skin in contact with different pressure zones. This embodiment embeds a network of highly sensitive, low-power thermistors and a micro-Peltier element array within the seat and backrest. By precisely measuring minute temperature differentials caused by localized blood flow changes due to posture-induced pressure, the system infers posture without relying on mechanical displacement or direct contact pressure.

newData.title: Spiro-Mechanical Posture Load Balancing Seat
newData.description: Instead of detecting posture, this embodiment actively balances occupant load using a pneumatically controlled diaphragm system that dynamically redistributes air pressure within a sealed, flexible seat cavity. Posture deviation causes predictable shifts in internal air pressure distribution, which are sensed by a series of low-profile, micro-differential pressure transducers. These transducers then actuate micro-valves to subtly adjust air flow between chambers, counteracting the deviation and promoting a balanced load distribution.

newData.title: Chiral-Phase Ergonomic Luminescence Emitter
newData.description: This design utilizes a novel chiral liquid crystal composite embedded within the chair's upholstery. When subjected to differential pressure from the user's posture, the chiral liquid crystal molecules undergo a predictable, reversible phase transition, altering their optical rotation. A low-power, directional light source (e.g., UV LED) illuminates the material, and a filtered photodetector array measures the intensity and wavelength shift of the reflected/transmitted light, which is directly correlated to the user's spinal curvature and pressure distribution.
